revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''進士'''（しんし）とは、律令制において式部省が行った秀才・明経に次ぐ第三の官吏登用試験。'''進士試'''（しんしし...'
revision.text:
'''進士'''（しんし）とは、律令制において式部省が行った秀才・明経に次ぐ第三の官吏登用試験。'''進士試'''（しんしし）とも。
博く群書に通じ才学の高い人物を登用するため、「治国之要務」（時事問題）を問う時務策と呼ばれる論文が2題と『[[文選_(書物)|文選]]』の中から7首、・『[[爾雅]]』の中から3首、合計10首の[[帖試]]（本文を暗記させて、出題者が示した空白部分を暗唱させる課題）を課した。甲乙丙の3段階で評価され、甲第と乙第に評定されると及第とされ、前者は従八位下、後者は大初位上に叙された。だが、及第には論文2題がともに文章・論旨ともに適格とされて合格と判断され、かつ帖試は甲第が全問、乙第が6問以上の正答を必要としたため、極めて難問であった。歴史上、甲第として知られる例は{{和暦|716}}に[[遣唐使|遣唐]][[留学生]]に選ばれた[[下道真備]]など数少ない。
秀才などに比較して及第者に対する叙位が低い一方で、出題が難問であったために志願者が少なく、平安時代の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]年間に廃止され、「進士」の呼称は[[漢文]]作成の試験で選抜された[[紀伝道]]学生である[[文章生]]に対する別称となった。
== 参考文献 == *野村忠夫「進士」（『国史大辞典 7』（吉川弘文館、1986年） ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4） *野村忠夫「進士」（『日本史大事典 3』（平凡社、1993年） ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1） *古藤真平「進士」（『日本歴史大事典 2』（小学館、2000年）ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3）

'''進士'''（しんし）とは、律令制において式部省が行った秀才・明経に次ぐ第三の官吏登用試験。'''進士試'''（しんしし）とも。
博く群書に通じ才学の高い人物を登用するため、「治国之要務」（時事問題）を問う時務策と呼ばれる論文が2題と『[[文選_(書物)|文選]]』の中から7首、・『[[爾雅]]』の中から3首、合計10首の[[帖試]]（本文を暗記させて、出題者が示した空白部分を暗唱させる課題）を課した。甲乙丙の3段階で評価され、甲第と乙第に評定されると及第とされ、前者は従八位下、後者は大初位上に叙された。だが、及第には論文2題がともに文章・論旨ともに適格とされて合格と判断され、かつ帖試は甲第が全問、乙第が6問以上の正答を必要としたため、極めて難問であった。歴史上、甲第として知られる例は{{和暦|716}}に[[遣唐使|遣唐]][[留学生]]に選ばれた[[下道真備]]など数少ない。
秀才などに比較して及第者に対する叙位が低い一方で、出題が難問であったために志願者が少なく、平安時代の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]年間に廃止され、「進士」の呼称は[[漢文]]作成の試験で選抜された[[紀伝道]]学生である[[文章生]]に対する別称となった。
== 参考文献 == *野村忠夫「進士」（『国史大辞典 7』（吉川弘文館、1986年） ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4） *野村忠夫「進士」（『日本史大事典 3』（平凡社、1993年） ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1） *古藤真平「進士」（『日本歴史大事典 2』（小学館、2000年）ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3）
{{japanese-history-stub}}

== 参考文献 == *野村忠夫「進士」（『国史大辞典 7』（吉川弘文館、1986年） ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4） *野村忠夫「進士」（『日本史大事典 3』（平凡社、1993年） ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1） *古藤真平「進士」（『日本歴史大事典 2』（小学館、2000年）ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3）
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.kyoto.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html フィールド・ミュージアム京都「大学寮址」]
== 関連項目 == *[[科挙]] *[[考試]] *[[進士]] *[[大学寮]] *[[紀伝道]] *[[漢字文化圏]]
{{japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi). == Overview == To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of st..."

revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== Related Topics == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

[[ja:進士]]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

revision.comment: some pages here lack the tag but to my knowledge everything here eitherr is an original page or at least worth preserving while I potentially trirm the wikipedia derived articles
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== Related Topics == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== Related Topics == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== Related Topics == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== Related Topics == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

[[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Jinshi (Japan)-qq]] [[Category:Jinshi (Japan)]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:進士]]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

(Japan)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:進士]]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).

However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were appointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were aqqointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were aqqointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were aqqointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

revision.text:

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
