'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).

Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3. == See Also! == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year }}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3. == See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year }}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3. == See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏}} {{Div col end}}

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year }}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Japanese Education]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Japanese Confucianism]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:Daigakuryō]] [[Category:Japanese Court Ranks]] [[Category:Academic Titles]] [[Category:Japanese-language Surnames]] [[Category:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[ja:進士 (日本)]] [[ja:進士]] [[sn:inshi (Japan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Jinshi (Japan)}}

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=draft}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=draft}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature|12=draft}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅|12=draft}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=draft}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使|12=draft}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year|12=draft}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=draft}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=draft}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|12=draft}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=draft}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=draft}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Japanese Education]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Japanese Confucianism]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:Daigakuryō]] [[Category:Japanese Court Ranks]] [[Category:Academic Titles]] [[Category:Japanese-language Surnames]] [[Category:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[ja:進士 (日本)]] [[ja:進士]] [[sn:inshi (Japan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Jinshi (Japan)}}

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=draft}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=draft}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature|12=draft}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅|12=draft}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=draft}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使|12=draft}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year|12=draft}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=draft}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=draft}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|12=draft}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=draft}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=draft}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏|12=draft}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

<!--Jinshi (Japan)--> {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Japanese Education]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Japanese Confucianism]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:Daigakuryō]] [[Category:Japanese Court Ranks]] [[Category:Academic Titles]] [[Category:Japanese-language Surnames]] [[Category:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Jinshi (Japan)]] [[ja:進士 (日本)]] [[ja:進士]] [[sn:inshi (Japan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Jinshi (Japan)}}

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial|ja|式部省|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=draft}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=draft}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wenxuan|ja|文選_(書物)|lt=Selections of Refined Literature|12=draft}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|ja|爾雅|12=draft}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=draft}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使|12=draft}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year|12=draft}}.
While the ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=draft}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観 (日本)|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=draft}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|12=draft}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=draft}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=draft}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=draft}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial Examination|ja|科挙|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinshi|ja|進士|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sinographic Sphere|ja|漢字文化圏|12=draft}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

revision.text:
'''進士'''（しんし）とは、[[律令制]]において[[式部省]]が行った[[秀才 (日本)|秀才]]・[[明経]]に次ぐ第三の官吏登用試験。'''進士試'''（しんしし）とも。
== 概説 == 博く群書に通じ才学の高い人物を登用するため、「治国之要務」（時事問題）を問う時務策と呼ばれる論文が2題と『[[文選_(書物)|文選]]』の中から7首、『[[爾雅]]』の中から3首、合計10首の[[帖試]]（本文を暗記させて、出題者が示した空白部分を暗唱させる課題）を課した。甲乙丙の3段階で評価され、甲第と乙第に評定されると及第とされ、前者は従八位下、後者は大初位上に叙された。だが、及第には論文2題がともに文章・論旨ともに適格とされて合格と判断され、かつ帖試は甲第が全問、乙第が6問以上の正答を必要としたため、極めて難問であった。歴史上、甲第として知られる例は[[716年]]（霊亀2年）に[[遣唐使|遣唐]][[留学生]]に選ばれた下道真備（[[吉備真備]]）など数少ない。また庶民から進士に合格し下級官人となり、最終的に貴族にまでなった人物として[[勇山文継]]が知られている。
秀才などに比較して及第者に対する叙位が低い一方で、出題が難問であったために志願者が少なく、平安時代の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]年間に廃止され、「進士」の呼称は[[漢文]]作成の試験で選抜された[[紀伝道]]学生である[[文章生]]に対する別称となった。
== 参考文献 == *[[野村忠夫]]「進士」『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]] 7』（[[吉川弘文館]] 1986年） ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4 *野村忠夫「進士」『[[日本史大事典]] 3』（[[平凡社]] 1993年） ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1 *[[古藤真平]]「進士」『[[日本歴史大事典]] 2』（[[小学館]] 2000年）ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3
== 関連項目 == {{Div col}} *[[科挙]] *[[考試]] *[[進士]] *[[大学寮]] *[[進士氏]] *[[紀伝道]] *[[漢字文化圏]] {{Div col end}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html フィールド・ミュージアム京都「大学寮址」]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんし}} [[Category:日本の教育の歴史]] [[Category:古代日本の教育機関]] [[Category:日本の儒学]] [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の位階]] [[Category:学術称号]] [[Category:日本語の姓]]

It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=}}.

However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:日本の律令制
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}

<!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--><!--Jinshi (Japan)--> [[Category:古代日本の教育機関]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:Academic honours]] [[Category:日本の位階]] [[Category:Confucianism in Japan]] [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:History of education in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese-language surnames]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:進士 (日本)]]

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Japanese Education
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大学寮]] into [[Category:University Dormitories]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:日本の位階-new]] into [[Category:日本の位階]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Greater Initial Rank|lt=Greater Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

<!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--> [[Category:Ancient Educational Institutions in Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Academic honours]] [[Category:Confucianism in Japan]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:History of education in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese-language surnames]] [[Category:Japanese ranks]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:進士 (日本)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|qq=|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|qq=|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|qq=|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}

To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|qq=|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|qq=|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]
