<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

<!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--> [[Category:Ancient Educational Institutions in Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Academic honours]] [[Category:Confucianism in Japan]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:History of education in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese-language surnames]] [[Category:Japanese ranks]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:進士 (日本)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11640137}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in [[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten]] 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * [[:en:Imperial examination|Imperial Examination]] * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * [[:en:Jinshi|Shinshi]] * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * [[:en:Sinosphere|Sinographic Sphere]] {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the [[:en:Shikibu-shō|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the [[:en:Wen Xuan|Selections of Refined Literature]] and three from the [[:en:Erya|Erya]]. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi no Makibi]], who was selected as a student for the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan era]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

<!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--note do not remove this category during translation--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--><!--Shinshi (Japan)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11640137}} [[Category:Academic honours]] [[Category:Ancient Educational Institutions in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Confucianism in Japan]] [[Category:History of education in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese ranks]] [[Category:Japanese-language surnames]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:進士 (日本)]][[zh:進士 (日本)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11640137]] Wikidata name: shinshi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|WD=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Meikyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wen Xuan|en|Wen Xuan|WD=Q1190957|lt=Selections of Refined Literature}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|en|Erya|WD=Q1207685|lt=Erya}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Shūsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan era}} in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|en|Kokushi Daijiten|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten}} 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986) ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. * Tadao Nomura, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * {{ill|Shinpei Kotō|ja|古藤真平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpei Kotō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "Shinshi," in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} 2 (Shōgakukan, 2000) ISBN 978-4-09-523002-3.
== See Also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|WD=Q7408|lt=Imperial Examination}} * {{ill|Kōshi|ja|考試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 課試|1=kashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631714}} * {{ill|Jinshi|en|Jinshi|WD=Q420477|lt=Shinshi}} * {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} * {{ill|Shinshishi|ja|進士氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshishi|1=Shinshishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640140}} * {{ill|Kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}} * {{ill|Sinosphere|en|Sinosphere|WD=Q716566|lt=Sinographic Sphere}} {{Div col end}}
== External Links == * [https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/somu/rekishi/fm/ishibumi/html/na008.html Field Museum Kyoto: "Site of Daigakuryō"]

Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Q11594542|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan era|1=Jōgan}} in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.

'''Shinshi''' (進士) refers to the third civil service examination conducted by the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|WD=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|1=Shikibu-shō}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, following {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Q11594543|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594543}} and {{ill|Meikyō|ja|明経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikyō|1=Q11512610|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512610}}. It is also called the '''Shinshi Examination''' (進士試, Shinshishi).
== Overview ==
To recruit individuals widely knowledgeable and scholarly, candidates were tested on two essays addressing "critical affairs of state" (治国之要務) called {{ill|Jimusaku|ja|時務策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimusaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as ten passages: seven from the {{ill|Wen Xuan|en|Wen Xuan|WD=Q1190957|lt=Selections of Refined Literature|1=Wen Xuan}} and three from the {{ill|Erya|en|Erya|WD=Q1207685|lt=Erya|1=Erya}}. This test involved {{ill|Chōshi|ja|帖試|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which required memorization and oral recitation of selected blank portions.
Performance was evaluated in three grades: "excellent" (甲), "good" (乙), and "passable" (丙). Candidates graded 甲 or 乙 were considered successful. Those achieving 甲 were a11ointed to the Rank of {{ill|Jugoi-no-Ge|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugoi-no-Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, while those achieving 乙 were granted {{ill|Daisoi-no-Jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisoi-no-Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}}. However, passing required flawless essays in terms of both content and reasoning, and a high standard for Chōshi responses (perfect for 甲, six or more correct for 乙). As a result, the exam was notoriously difficult. Notable examples of 甲 achievers include {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}}, who was selected as a student for the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} mission in {{ill|716 (Reiki 2)|ja|霊亀2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/716 (Reiki 2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
While the Ranks awarded to successful candidates were lower than those of {{ill|Shūsai|ja|秀才|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūsai|1=Q11594542|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594542}} and other examinations, the high difficulty resulted in low participation. Consequently, the Shinshi system was abolished during the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan era|1=Jōgan}} in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Thereafter, the term "Shinshi" referred to {{ill|Bunshōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97378717}} (students of historiography) selected through tests in classical Chinese composition.
